Transcrição Completa: 43 Money Is In The Follow Up

 

[00:00]Your money, your potential deals are in the followup. Okay? As soon as you send one of the perfect cold video pitches, or maybe you're doing something more ineffective like cold calling, uh maybe you're sending 60-second cold videos via Instagram DMs or Facebook Messenger or LinkedIn, whatever you're using to increase your outflow.

 

[00:22]Once again, the money is going to be in the follow-up, though. Okay? So, you're you're gonna have a a statistically lower odd to get more clients if you're solely reliant upon one call closes.

 

[00:35]So, if you're sending the perfect cold video pitch out and you're expecting out of every hundred people that you send that to to have maybe one or two that'll just be like, "Hey, this makes perfect sense. Let's go ahead and do a deal." That's statistically possible.

 

[00:50]But if you want to be closing 10, 20 people for every hundred people that you send the perfect code video pitch to, then the money is in the follow-up after you send that initial communication.

 

[01:03]My old boss, Grant Cardone, he claimed that it was between five and 12 follow-ups that you can have a higher probability for closing a deal. And I completely agree if they're quality communications.

 

[01:16]But I find nowadays, late 2018, 2019 and onward, it's tougher to get in communication with people depending on where you're communicating with them.

 

[01:26]In my sales process. We're closing high ticket deals. We're closing people between 10K and 50K plus a month. And they are high ticket, very high net worth individuals. So, they're also harder to communicate with.

 

[01:41]We constantly find ourselves having to communicate through people that are around them to get to the key decision maker. And in that process, that increases the amount of communications and follow-ups that we're going to have.

 

[01:52]In total, we find ourselves delivering valuable information to people between about 10 to sometimes I've had deals that have taken up to 80 different follow-up attempts.

 

[02:04]Now, let's break this down for what it really means when I say between 10 and 80 follow-up attempts so you don't get all overwhelmed and freaked out.

 

[02:13]That means something as simple as what you're probably already doing every day, going on social media, DMing people, or telling chicks they're hot. You have so much attention that you're already putting onto social media.

 

[02:26]I bet if you have iOS 12, you're probably getting the the weekly reports called screen time that tell you how much you're using your phone. I bet you're using that sucker hours a day.

 

[02:37]So, where you currently put your attention, instead of doing all the scrolling and the participating on social media, you're officially going to leverage it to make you money by using it to follow up with people that are actively in your pipeline.

 

[02:51]I promise you, it's a lot more fun to make tens of thousands of dollars in a month off social media than it is to just go and scroll through it and like some and comment. Hear me out.

 

[03:01]The money's in the follow-up. And it's so simple to send a little 10 to 15 second video here and there. It's so simple to go grab an article that relates to the person's industry that you're trying to close with something that furthers what you're talking about, what you're trying to prove, objections you're trying to handle, beliefs you need the person to have that you could share with them.

 

[03:24]Say, "Hey, I saw this article. Thought of you." Send it over to them. There are so many different things you could record on your computer to break down the process of what you're trying to sell to this person to help them understand it to leverage the power of visual aids.

 

[03:39]Send them a quick two-minute video as you choose to send valuable follow-up. Not a bunch of annoying, hey, when can we talk on the phone? Hey, I'd love to get some of your time. Did you see the last video that I sent over? I'm wondering when we can talk.

 

[03:54]You know, we have I had did you see my last messages? Nobody's going to respond to you because those are annoying messages. Would you respond to yourself is always the number one rule of thumb and not in a way where you're lying to yourself.

 

[04:08]In a way where you have in your head an idea of real standards of business owners. Think about this. When you're doing sales, you're interrupting people. They have no idea who you are.

 

[04:19]They have an entire day planned of activities that are usually revenue generating, especially when they're a business owner. And here comes whatever your name is sending them a video or following up in their DMs or hitting them in their email.

 

[04:32]You better have something valuable that helps make them more revenue and drive them more leads, not something that's just an annoying ass, hey, when can we talk on the phone? And you've delivered no value, made nothing of what that time is going to be used for available to the person to make a decision with.

 

[04:49]Talk about an agenda if you're going to ask for a phone call and what you're going to talk about and why. You know, explain to me as a business owner how it's going to make me more money, drive me more leads. Think about how you're disrespecting my time.

 

[05:03]Empathetically say something like, "I would like to earn every second of our conversation. You can hang up on me anytime. Give me two minutes. I want to explain this process whether we work together or not. It'll make you more money, drive you more leads."

 

[05:18]Consider valuable followup when you're in the process of your 10 to 80 plus communications after you've sent the initial cold video pitch. Don't get bogged down if people don't respond to you either.

 

[05:30]If you guys want in the Facebook group, my initial salesperson, he's been with me for over three three years now, three plus years. His name's Leo Guron, okay? You can go talk to him.

 

[05:42]He's been through all of it with me, growing the business, scaling up the sales teams, and more importantly, pitching and closing some of the world's largest personality brands right alongside of me.

 

[05:53]Him and I constantly role play with one another trying to figure out what the next best follow-up medium is. And we found 60-second videos, even 15 second videos, recording the process on your computer screen.

 

[06:06]Bust out QuickTime if you have a Mac. Do a little screen recording. Get a nice mic. You can also use Camtasia, whatever other screen recording software you have. Just demonstrate what your service does.

 

[06:18]There's so many different ways. Show the targeting if you're advertising. Show what they can do with Facebook audiences or or Instagram audiences, right? Show people what they can do for how they can retarget more people from their email list or what the possibilities are with the email list they already have.

 

[06:35]Break down the process and the concept. Show people case studies that you've already done. Have them get on the phone with another client that you have. Do anything to follow up and provide value to people because that's where the money's at.

 

[06:49]All right, I'll see you in the next

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

1-2 pessoas em 100 fecham no primeiro contato (1-2% conversão inicial)

10-20 pessoas em 100 podem fechar com follow-up adequado (10-20% conversão com follow-up)

5-12 follow-ups recomendados por Grant Cardone

10-80+ follow-up attempts na prática (casos reais)

$10K-$50K+ por mês - Valor dos deals high ticket mencionados

10-15 segundos - Duração de vídeos curtos de follow-up

60 segundos - Duração de vídeos de follow-up mais elaborados

2 minutos - Duração de vídeos explicativos detalhados

3+ anos - Tempo de experiência de Leo Guron (salesperson principal)

iOS 12 - Screen Time reports mencionados

Tens of thousands of dollars por mês possíveis via social media

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Perfect Cold Video Pitch como método inicial de outreach

Follow-up como principal gerador de receita (não one-call-closes)

Múltiplos canais: Instagram DMs, Facebook Messenger, LinkedIn, Email

Comunicação através de gatekeepers para chegar ao decision maker

Valuable Follow-up vs Annoying Follow-up (diferenciação crítica)

Leverage de tempo em social media para follow-ups produtivos

Screen Time awareness para redirecionar atenção

Role playing constante entre equipe de vendas

Demonstrações visuais via screen recording (QuickTime, Camtasia)

Compartilhamento de artigos relevantes da indústria

Case studies como ferramenta de follow-up

Client testimonials via phone calls

Vídeos de 10-15 segundos para touches rápidos

Vídeos de 60 segundos para explicações médias

Vídeos de 2 minutos para breakdowns detalhados

Agenda clara ao pedir phone calls

Abordagem empática: "earn every second", "you can hang up anytime"

Foco em "make more money, drive more leads" em toda comunicação

 

ESTRUTURA:

Framework Grant Cardone: 5-12 follow-ups para maior probabilidade de fechamento

Framework Jeremy Haynes: 10-80+ follow-ups para high ticket deals

Pipeline ativo de prospects em follow-up constante

High Net Worth Individuals = mais gatekeepers = mais follow-ups necessários

Regra de ouro: "Would you respond to yourself?" (self-awareness test)

Respeito ao tempo do business owner (revenue-generating activities)

Value-first approach em todos os follow-ups

Multi-touch, multi-channel strategy

Sales team structure: Leo Guron como primeiro salesperson (3+ anos)

Role playing culture para otimização de métodos

Facebook group como community de suporte

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

$10,000-$50,000+ por mês por cliente (high ticket deals)

Conversão: 1-2% sem follow-up vs 10-20% com follow-up adequado

ROI de tempo: Tens of thousands of dollars/mês via social media follow-ups

High net worth individuals como target market

World's largest personality brands como clientes fechados

Múltiplos anos de receita sustentável (3+ anos com mesmo salesperson)

 

DURAÇÃO: ~7:00 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional direto para câmera, sem slides visuais detectados, tom conversacional e direto
